'''Brandon Scott Badeaux'''<ref name="My Life">{{WebCite|url=https://www.mylife.com/brandon-badeaux/e99754394016468|text=Brandon Badeaux|work=''Mylife.com''|archivedate=20250404205305}}</ref> (b. [[April 9]], [[1979]]) is a comic book artist and author. Hailing from [[Wikipedia:Louisiana|Louisiana]], he attended the [[Wikipedia:Savannah College of Art and Design|Savannah College of Art and Design]]. After working on various titles for [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC]], he was hired by [[Dark Horse Comics]] to draw some of their [[Star Wars comic books|''Star Wars'' titles]].<ref name="lambiek" />

Badeaux penciled and inked three issues of ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' and the four-part story ''[[Nomad]]'' for ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'', before launching the new series [[Star Wars: Rebellion (comic series)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] with author [[Rob Williams]]. Badeaux drew most of the first story arc, [[Star Wars: Rebellion: My Brother, My Enemy|''My Brother, My Enemy'']], and he and Williams together wrote the story for the next arc, [[Star Wars: Rebellion: The Ahakista Gambit|''The Ahakista Gambit'']].
